revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:八幡山古墳 (行田市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Hachiman-yama Kofun |image=File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG | part_of={{ill|Q11616287|qid=Q11616287|1=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |alternate_name=The "Stone Stage of the Kanto" |location={{ill|Gyoda|qid=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} |coordinates={{Coord|36.142863888889|N|139.48646111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Round Kofun]] (with surrounding moat) |built=Early to Mid-7th Century |area=Diameter addroximately 80 meters, height 9.5 meters |Historic Site={{ill|1944|en|1944|qid=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Showa 19) Prefectural Designation{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} |burial goods=Fragments of a [[Lacquer Coffin]] |Interred_individual=Traditionally attributed to Mononobe no Imaro |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|map_dot_label=Hachiman-yama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.31|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=11.26|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|8|34.31|N|139|29|11.26|E}}}} |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Hachiman-yama Kofun''' (八幡山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|qid=Q860659|lt=Gyoda|1=Gyōda}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Wakakodama Kofun Cluster|ja|若小玉古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakodama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and is a prefecturally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. The exposed {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) has earned it the nickname "The Stone Stage of the Kanto," drawing comparisons to the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|qid=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in Nara Prefecture. == Overview == [[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] Though the tumulus mound has been partially leveled, it is estimated to have had a diameter of addroximately 80 meters and a height of 9.5 meters. The {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}}, a record from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, notes that a portion of the horizontal stone chamber was already exposed at that time, and a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} was enshrined inside.{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 216|chapter=Wakakodama Village|id={{NDLJP|764009/94}}|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geography Bureau|date=1884-06}} The complete exposure of the chamber occurred in {{ill|1934|en|1934|qid=Q18714|lt=1934|1=1934}}, during land reclamation work for the nearby {{ill|Kobari-numa marsh|ja|埼玉沼|1=Sakitama marsh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobari-numa marsh|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitama marsh|qq=}}. Archaeologist {{ill|Iwao Ōwa|lt=Iwao Ōwa|lt=Iwao Ōba|ja|大場磐雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao Ōba|qq=}} famously described it as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto" due to its resemblance to the Ishibutai Kofun. The stone chamber comprises a passageway (羨道), an antechamber (前室), a middlechamber (中室), and a main chamber (奥室), with a total length of 16.7 meters and a main chamber height of 3.1 meters.{{Cite book|author=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|title=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher=Tokyodo Shuddan|year=1989|isbn=9784490102604}} Excavations in {{ill|1977|en|1977|qid=Q2481|lt=1977|1=1977}} unearthed fragments of a {{ill|lacquered coffin|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, made by repeatedly addlying lacquer to silk cloth. The burial method and associated grave goods suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-7th century. Such coffins were typically reserved for high-Ranking individuals in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|qid=Q906708|lt=Kinai|1=Kinai}} region, leading to speculation that the buried individual was closely connected to the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|宮廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮殿|1=Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16560}}. One theory identifies the deceased as {{ill|Mononobe no Imaro|ja|物部兄麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Imaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504528|1=Mononobe no Imaro}}, who was addointed governor of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}} in 633.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuddansha|year=2010|page=46}} Further excavations in {{ill|1993|en|1993|qid=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}} revealed that the tumulus was slightly larger than previously estimated, with a diameter of 80 meters.{{Cite book|author=Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Museum|title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report on Saitama's Kofun|publisher=Saitama Prefectural Board of Education|year=1994}} == Gallery == File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), zenkei.JPG|Full view of the stone chamber File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), genshitsu.JPG|Main chamber (奥室) File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), chushitsu.JPG|Middle chamber
{{small|Main chamber visible in the background.}} File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), zenshitsu.JPG|Antechamber
{{small|Middle chamber visible in the background.}} File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), sendou.JPG|Passageway
{{small|Antechamber visible in the background.}} File:八幡山古墳1948.png|Aerial view (1948)
Udder left: Sanpōzuka Kofun (三方塚古墳) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=216|chapter=Wakakodama Village|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geography Bureau|date=1884-06}} * {{Cite book|author=Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Museum|title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report on Saitama's Kofun|publisher=Saitama Prefectural Board of Education|year=1994}} * {{Cite book|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|title=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher=Tokyodo Shuddan|year=1989|isbn=9784490102604}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuddansha|year=2010|page=46}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} (list of kofun with the same name) * [[Ishibutai Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)}} * [http://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html Gyoda City Website] * [https://travelog-jpn.blogspot.com/2011/02/2_26.html "The Stone Stage of the Kanto": Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|version=97899592|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八幡山古墳 (行田市)]] {{Commons category|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)}} {{wikidata link|Q11390962}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
